headlines.title: BLM Expands Oil Leasing Drive with July Auction Plans
headlines.summary: The Bureau of Land Management is teeing up another round of federal oil and gas leasing, with 66 parcels across Montana and North Dakota headed for auction in July as Washington continues pushing a noticeably more drill-friendly agenda. headlines.title: Ottawa and Alberta Push New 1 Million Bpd Oil Pipeline
headlines.summary: Canada may finally be doing the thing everyone has been yelling about for the last decade: building more oil pipelines. headlines.title: Russia’s Oil Windfall Gets Bigger as Hormuz Stays Shut
headlines.summary: Russia just found itself on the right side of the oil shock. headlines.title: China's Gasoline Consumption Could Plunge 5.5% in 2026 as Oil Prices Surge
headlines.summary: China's gasoline demand has dropped since the Iran war upended the global oil markets and is on track to decline more than previously expected this year, due to the higher prices and the continued push toward electric vehicles. headlines.title: Sinopec Opens Major Ultra-Deep Shale Gas Play in China
headlines.summary: Chinese state oil giant Sinopec is opening a major ultra-deep shale gas find after obtaining official government approval for proven geological reserves of 235.687 billion cubic meters in the Ziyang Dongfeng field in the Sichuan province. headlines.title: Global Oil Stockpiles Plunge as Iran War Chokes Supply
headlines.summary: The West’s international energy watchdog has warned that oil stockpiles were being drained at a record rate last month as the US’s war in Iran continues to choke supply in an “unprecedented” supply shock.
